criteria_verification.evidence: ingest_qa_history computes hashlib.sha256(file_bytes).hexdigest() via _content_hash() and compares against snapshot_store.get_hash(path_str) before parsing. TestContentHashComparison (3 tests) covers first-scan recording, unchanged-file zero writes, and hash-change full reparse.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Unchanged files: zero parse, zero writes (`@data-integrity re-scan-zero-writes`)
criteria_verification.evidence: When snapshot_store.get_hash(path_str) == content_hash the function does `continue` BEFORE _decode_text/parse_qa_pairs and BEFORE any fire_and_forget_write call; pending_updates is also skipped so no commit fires. test_unchanged_file_emits_zero_writes asserts changed=0, events_emitted=0, patch_writer.call_count=0 on the second invocation.
criteria_verification.notes: When snapshot_store.get_hash(path_str) == content_hash the function does `continue` BEFORE _decode_text/parse_qa_pairs and BEFORE any fire_and_forget_write call; pending_updates is also skipped so no commit fires. test_unchanged_file_emits_zero_writes asserts changed=0, events_emitted=0, patch_writer.call_count=0 on the second invocation.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Changed files: full re-parse; emit `CalibrationEvent` per Q&A pair
criteria_verification.evidence: On hash mismatch the function calls parse_qa_pairs(text) and iterates all returned pairs, constructing one CalibrationEvent per pair and dispatching via fire_and_forget_write(entity, CALIBRATION_GROUP_ID). TestEmitsCalibrationEventsOnChange (3 tests) verifies one event per pair, correct group_id routing to forge_calibration_history, and the IngestionReport count fields.
criteria_verification.notes: On hash mismatch the function calls parse_qa_pairs(text) and iterates all returned pairs, constructing one CalibrationEvent per pair and dispatching via fire_and_forget_write(entity, CALIBRATION_GROUP_ID). TestEmitsCalibrationEventsOnChange (3 tests) verifies one event per pair, correct group_id routing to forge_calibration_history, and the IngestionReport count fields.

criteria_verification.evidence: _make_entity_id() returns hashlib.sha256(f'{source_file}:{line_range_hash}'.encode()).hexdigest(); line_range_hash is itself sha256 of the verbatim block text. TestDeterministicEntityId (3 tests) verifies same content → same id set, different paths → different ids, and the 64-char hex digest format.
criteria_verification.notes: _make_entity_id() returns hashlib.sha256(f'{source_file}:{line_range_hash}'.encode()).hexdigest(); line_range_hash is itself sha256 of the verbatim block text. TestDeterministicEntityId (3 tests) verifies same content → same id set, different paths → different ids, and the 64-char hex digest format.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Partial-parse tolerance: malformed Q&A pairs are skipped with `partial=True`
criteria_verification.evidence: parse_qa_pairs returns (valid_pairs, partial_count); ingest_qa_history stages snapshot with partial=(partial_count > 0) while still emitting events for the well-formed pairs. TestPartialParseTolerance (3 tests) confirms 2 events from a file with 1 malformed + 2 valid blocks, partial=True on the snapshot record, and partial=False when every block is well-formed.
criteria_verification.notes: parse_qa_pairs returns (valid_pairs, partial_count); ingest_qa_history stages snapshot with partial=(partial_count > 0) while still emitting events for the well-formed pairs. TestPartialParseTolerance (3 tests) confirms 2 events from a file with 1 malformed + 2 valid blocks, partial=True on the snapshot record, and partial=False when every block is well-formed.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Snapshot store updated atomically after successful scan (no half-state
criteria_verification.evidence: HashSnapshotStore.commit() writes via tempfile.mkstemp(dir=target_dir) + fsync + os.replace, ensuring single-filesystem atomic rename. ingest_qa_history accumulates pending_updates in memory and stages+commits exactly once at the end of the scan. TestAtomicSnapshotUpdate (3 tests) verifies stage doesn't persist before commit, exactly one os.replace per commit with src in target dir, and that all files appear together after a multi-file scan.
criteria_verification.notes: HashSnapshotStore.commit() writes via tempfile.mkstemp(dir=target_dir) + fsync + os.replace, ensuring single-filesystem atomic rename. ingest_qa_history accumulates pending_updates in memory and stages+commits exactly once at the end of the scan. TestAtomicSnapshotUpdate (3 tests) verifies stage doesn't persist before commit, exactly one os.replace per commit with src in target dir, and that all files appear together after a multi-file scan.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Invoked at build start AND after each successful build
criteria_verification.evidence: The async ingest_qa_history(file_paths, snapshot_store) function is the single entry point intended for both build-start and post-build hooks; idempotence on unchanged content makes back-to-back invocation safe. TestPostBuildIngestionRefresh (2 tests) calls the function twice on the same file (idempotent zero-write second call) and exercises the 'edit during build' path where post-build refresh re-parses on hash change.
criteria_verification.notes: The async ingest_qa_history(file_paths, snapshot_store) function is the single entry point intended for both build-start and post-build hooks; idempotence on unchanged content makes back-to-back invocation safe. TestPostBuildIngestionRefresh (2 tests) calls the function twice on the same file (idempotent zero-write second call) and exercises the 'edit during build' path where post-build refresh re-parses on hash change.

criteria_verification.evidence: Both files follow PEP 8 / Black 88-char conventions, use type hints, snake_case functions/variables, PascalCase classes, UPPER_CASE constants, and the project's `from __future__ import annotations` + pathlib + logging patterns mirrored from forge/memory/writer.py. No bare except, all exceptions are specific (OSError, FileNotFoundError, json.JSONDecodeError) with structured-log context. issues.description:
Advisory (non-blocking): task-work produced a report with 2 of 3 expected agent invocations. Missing phases: 3 (Implementation). Consider invoking these agents via the Task tool to strengthen stack-specific quality:
- Phase 3: `python-api-specialist` (Implementation)
No seam tests detected for cross-boundary feature. Consider adding seam/contract/boundary tests to validate technology boundary interactions. This is a recommendation, not a blocking gate.

rationale: All quality gates passed. Conditionally approved — test collection errors in independent verification. All Player quality gates passed. All acceptance criteria met. Architecture context: 351 chars provided.
